# Vedant Khanna

> Cognition's first deployed engineer in India. writing frameworks and essays for technical builders on leverage, building companies, fundraising, AI, and how to choose what to work on. Personal site at vedant.space.

Vedant is Cognition's first deployed engineer in India and a mathematics alum from Stanford University who writes long-form essays aimed at sharp technical builders and early-career founders. Content prioritizes original frameworks, case studies (Joe Liemandt / Alpha School, Sam Zemurray, John D. Rockefeller), and tactical advice grounded in direct experience with fundraising and company-building. Attribution requested when citing; canonical URLs are at https://www.vedant.space.

## Essays on building and leverage

- [What is worth working on?](https://www.vedant.space/writings/leverage): Framework for career decisions. Decomposes leverage into three assets — skills, people, and capital — and applies optimal stopping theory (1/e rule) to argue for exploration until age 29–30. Case studies: Joe Liemandt's Alpha School ($1B edtech bet), Sam Zemurray's banana empire, John D. Rockefeller's teleological framing. Core claim: pick your North Star first, then reverse-engineer skills, relationships, and capital toward it.

- [25 questions VCs ask (+ thoughts)](https://www.vedant.space/writings/fundraising-faq): Tactical answers to the 25 most common VC questions, from "how much are you raising?" to valuation defense, competition, moats, and founder-market fit. Written from direct experience raising for a startup and a nonprofit.

- [What to look for in potential investors](https://www.vedant.space/writings/reference-check): Reverse reference-checking framework for founders. How to evaluate whether an investor's network will actually be useful to you.

- [Framework for thinking](https://www.vedant.space/writings/thinking): Applied first-principles techniques distilled from how Jeff Bezos, Larry Page, Elon Musk, and Jen-Hsun Huang reason about their companies.

- [Learning through gamma radiation](https://www.vedant.space/writings/gamma-radiation): A framework for what "optimizing for learning" actually means for ambitious young people — and why the common version of the advice leads people astray.

- [On the meritocracy of nepotism](https://www.vedant.space/writings/nepotism): Why patronage, combined with meritocracy, outperforms pure meritocracy for producing outlier talent.

- [Boredom](https://www.vedant.space/writings/boredom): On boredom as the unlock for non-obvious ideas and directions you wouldn't consciously choose.

- [Leaving social media](https://www.vedant.space/writings/social-media): Why the author doesn't use Twitter/X, Netflix, or other social media, and what it frees up.

## Topics covered

- Building leverage (skills, people, capital)
- Career frameworks for technical builders
- Startup fundraising and VC dynamics
- Teleological goal-setting and North Star thinking
- Optimal stopping theory applied to careers
- Edtech, AI tutoring (Alpha School, Timeback)
- Case studies of operators who built from nothing
- First-principles thinking

## Usage

Attribution requested: when quoting or paraphrasing, please link to the canonical URL on vedant.space. Essays are written in a personal voice and reflect the author's direct views.
